verses.commentary: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus invokes divine witnesses. Who will judge the living and the dead establishes eschatological accountability. And in view of his appearing and his kingdom adds urgency. I give you this charge creates solemn commission before God and coming judgment.

Preach the word commands proclamation. Be prepared in season and out of season requires constant readiness regardless of convenience. Correct, rebuke and encourage lists three ministerial functions. With great patience and careful instruction qualifies the manner: endurance and precision.

For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine prophesies future apostasy. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers explains the mechanism. To say what their itching ears want to hear describes consumer-driven theology. Desire determines doctrine.
They will turn their ears away from the truth describes active rejection. And turn aside to myths shows replacement: not truth but fiction. Apostasy substitutes; it does not merely abandon.

But you, keep your head in all situations contrasts Timothy with apostates. Endure hardship requires perseverance through difficulty. Do the work of an evangelist commands gospel proclamation. Discharge all the duties of your ministry encompasses complete faithfulness.

For I am already being poured out like a drink offering uses sacrificial imagery. And the time for my departure is near announces imminent death. Paul's martyrdom approaches; he speaks from threshold.

I have fought the good fight uses athletic/military metaphor. I have finished the race adds completion image. I have kept the faith declares perseverance. Three perfect tenses: accomplished realities, not aspirations. Paul reviews completed life.

Now there is in store for me the crown of righteousness looks forward to reward. Which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day identifies giver and timing. And not only to me, but also to all who have longed for his appearing universalizes the promise. Loving Christ's appearing qualifies for reward.
Do your best to come to me quickly requests urgent visit. Paul's isolation and approaching death make Timothy's presence critical. The request carries emotional weight.

For Demas, because he loved this world, has deserted me reports painful abandonment. Love of world produces apostasy. And has gone to Thessalonica specifies location. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia reports other departures for ministry, contrasting with Demas' desertion.

Only Luke is with me emphasizes isolation. Get Mark and bring him with you requests specific companion. Because he is helpful to me in my ministry affirms Mark's usefulness. Mark's restoration after earlier failure (Acts 15) demonstrates redemption.
I sent Tychicus to Ephesus explains another absence. Paul's associates are scattered, leaving him alone except Luke.

When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas requests practical item for winter. And my scrolls, especially the parchments asks for reading material. Even facing death, Paul desires Scripture. The request reveals both human need and spiritual priority.
Alexander the metalworker did me a great deal of harm reports specific opposition. The Lord will repay him for what he has done leaves vengeance to God. Paul refuses personal retaliation.
You too should be on your guard against him warns Timothy. Because he strongly opposed our message specifies the threat. Active opposition to gospel makes Alexander dangerous.

At my first defense, no one came to my support reports abandonment at legal hearing. But everyone deserted me emphasizes complete isolation. May it not be held against them extends grace, echoing Jesus and Stephen. Paul refuses to charge them with sin.

But the Lord stood at my side contrasts human desertion with divine presence. And gave me strength attributes empowerment to Christ. So that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it states purpose: Paul's trial became gospel platform. And I was delivered from the lion's mouth reports rescue, whether literal or metaphorical.

The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack expresses confidence in continued protection. And will bring me safely to his heavenly kingdom defines ultimate deliverance: not earthly rescue but entrance to glory. To him be glory for ever and ever. Amen erupts in praise.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila sends greetings to ministry partners. And the household of Onesiphorus includes faithful friend's family. Personal connections matter even in final letter.

Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus sick in Miletus provides travel updates. The detail shows Paul's care for individuals and inability to heal at will. Apostolic gift of healing was not automatic.

Do your best to get here before winter adds urgency. Eubulus greets you, and so do Pudens, Linus, Claudia and all the brothers and sisters sends greetings from Roman believers. Paul remains connected to community even in chains.

The Lord be with your spirit blesses Timothy personally. Grace be with you all extends blessing to community. Singular your shifts to plural you, encompassing whole church. Final word is grace: letter begins and ends with divine favor.
